title: Shri Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple
intro: The Shri Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple, located on the holy banks of the Narmada River in the Khandwa district of Madhya Pradesh, is a famous pilgrimage site dedicated to Lord Shiva.
description:
Shri Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple – Madhya Pradesh
Introduction
The Shri Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple, located on the holy banks of the Narmada River in the Khandwa district of Madhya Pradesh, is a famous pilgrimage site dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva.
The temple is situated on Mandhata Island, which, according to religious belief, is shaped like the sacred symbol 'ॐ' (Om). This place is a major center of faith, peace, and spiritual experience for devotees.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple
 Other Names: Omkareshwar Dham, Omkar Mahadev Temple
 Location: Mandhata Island, Khandwa District, Madhya Pradesh 450554, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Shiva
 Significance: One of the 12 major Jyotirlingas
 Nearest Major City: Indore (approx. 80 km)
History and Significance
Omkareshwar is one of the most sacred abodes of Lord Shiva. According to religious beliefs, Lord Shiva resides here in the form of a Jyotirlinga.
Situated along the banks of the Narmada River, this region has been a center of meditation and spiritual traditions since ancient times. The unique 'Om' shape of Mandhata Island is a special religious identifier for this place, and millions of devotees visit here to worship Lord Shiva and take a holy dip in the Narmada.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Devi Ahilyabai Holkar Airport, Indore: Distance from the temple is approx. 80 km. You can reach Omkareshwar from Indore by taxi or bus.
🚆 By Train
 Omkareshwar Road Railway Station: Distance from the temple is approx. 12 km. Taxis and local transport are available from the station.
🛣️ By Road
Omkareshwar is well-connected by road to Indore, Ujjain, and Khandwa. You can reach here easily by bus, taxi, or private vehicle.
Best Time to Visit
The Omkareshwar Temple remains open for devotees throughout the year.
Best time for the Yatra:
October to March, as the weather is more favorable for travel during this time.
Main Festivals
 Mahashivratri: A major festival for the special worship and celebration of Lord Shiva.
 Shravan Maas: Special worship of Lord Shiva is performed during the holy month of Sawan, drawing a large number of devotees.
Temple Timings
🕒 5:00 AM to 10:00 PM
(Timings may change on special occasions and festivals.)
Nearby Attractions
 Mamleshwar Temple: A famous Shiva temple and religious site in the Omkareshwar region.
 Narmada River: One of the holiest rivers in India, on whose banks Omkareshwar is situated.
 Om-shaped Island: Mandhata Island is famous for its unique 'Om' shape.
 Siddhanath Temple: A temple known for its ancient architecture and religious importance.
Dress Code
For temple visits:
 Wearing traditional or modest clothing is considered appropriate.
 Maintain respectful attire in accordance with the religious environment.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan arrangements
 Puja facilities
 Prasad facilities
 Accommodation arrangements
 Local transport facilities
 Help centers
Conclusion
The Shri Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple is one of the holiest Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. Located on the banks of the Narmada River, this Dham is famous for its religious significance, natural beauty, and spiritual atmosphere. A pilgrimage to Omkareshwar is a special opportunity for devotees to attain faith, peace, and a spiritual experience of Lord Shiva.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Omkareshwar Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: The Omkareshwar Temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.
faq.q2: Where is the Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga located?
faq.a2: It is located on Mandhata Island in the Khandwa district of Madhya Pradesh.
faq.a3: Omkareshwar is situated on the banks of the Narmada River.
faq.q4: Which is the nearest major city to the Omkareshwar Temple?
faq.a4: Indore is the nearest major city.
faq.q5: What is the best time to visit Omkareshwar?
faq.a5: The period from October to March is considered ideal for the pilgrimage.
